title: Balkholme (2) Halifax Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flt Lt. Maurice Charles Day

external_narratives.summary: Halifax MZ696 (coded LK-K) of 578 Squadron collided with Halifax LK834 at 2,000 feet while returning from Bottrop on 21 July 1944. Both crashed at Balkholme with all crew killed. MZ696 was piloted by Flt Lt Maurice Charles Day.
key_details: Mid-air collision during return from Bottrop raid

external_narratives.summary: The IWM lists the joint memorial for both Halifax crews lost in the Balkholme collision, with MZ696's crew names inscribed alongside those of LK834.
key_details: Joint memorial covers both aircraft crews

external_narratives.summary: 578 Squadron records document MZ696 as one of six aircraft lost from the squadron during the Bottrop raid of 20/21 July 1944, representing one of the heaviest single-night losses for the unit.
key_details: One of six 578 Sqn aircraft lost that night
Heaviest single-night loss for the squadron
